source: Derived from patterns across CB SAT Practice Tests 1-4 (data/official/SAT/)

mistakeCategories.name: Confusing slope with y-intercept
mistakeCategories.description: Student picks the y-intercept value as the rate of change.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: On a line with slope 20 and intercept 50, says 'the rate is 50'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Phrase the distractor as if the starting value were the per-unit rate.

mistakeCategories.name: Reading the per-unit rate from the starting value
mistakeCategories.description: Student treats the value at x=0 as the slope.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: On a line starting at 50K, says 'gains 50K per month'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Use the y-intercept number in the per-unit rate language.

mistakeCategories.name: Quoting an endpoint as if it were the rate
mistakeCategories.description: Student selects a specific (x, y) point and presents it as the rate.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Picks (20, 250) and says 'after 20 months the value is 250'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Describe a specific endpoint rather than rate of change.

mistakeCategories.name: Sign-reversed rate of change
mistakeCategories.description: Student calculates the absolute change correctly but mis-attributes direction (gain vs loss).
mistakeCategories.studentExample: On a line falling from 250 to 50 over 10 months, says 'gains 20 per month'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Reverse the direction of the rate while keeping the magnitude correct.

mistakeCategories.name: Slope correct but units mis-stated
mistakeCategories.description: Student computes the slope correctly but reports it in the wrong unit (e.g. thousands as units).
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Slope is 20 thousand per month, but student reports 20 per month.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Quote the slope value with the wrong order-of-magnitude unit suffix.
